summary: Independent QA accepts the M7 formal content lock Human Gate package as evidence-backed, process-only, and truthful about carried limits. This ACCEPT is package acceptance only: formal content lock is not approved, the task must not be closed as content locked, and explicit product-owner decision remains required.

decisions.decision: Accept the Human Gate review package as truthful and evidence-backed, but do not approve formal content lock.
Formal content lock acceptance remains an R4 Human Gate decision for product_owner: APPROVE_CONTENT_LOCK_WITH_LIMITS, DEFER_CONTENT_LOCK, or REQUEST_CHANGES.

Committed branch diff is confined to allowed process, message, task, goal-mode, model-routing, and thread-registry paths; no forbidden business, content-source, CI, roadmap, gate, manifest, checksum, or release artifact path changed.

The package preserves M6_GATE=PASS_WITH_LIMITS and does not rewrite Alpha/local evidence, batch-balance, adviser, prior M4/M5 process limits, or unsigned desktop smoke limitations into release readiness.
DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE remains in force; the package creates no M7/M8 manual node battle implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.
M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 is CLOSED and has both systems_architect ACCEPT and qa_reviewer ACCEPT evidence; the package still treats it as prerequisite evidence only.

The package carries Edge/Firefox tooling blocks, Windows DPI 150% follow-up, low-end Windows proxy limits, agent-operated manual playthrough limits, production culture/art limits, cloud-save/release/commercial/telemetry/server/mod gates, and major cultural-risk Human Gates.

risks.summary: QA accepts only the package; no product-owner approval is recorded, so content lock remains not accepted and autonomous continuation must stay paused at this decision point.
Lead and any later gate must not convert this QA ACCEPT into M7 Gate PASS, release readiness, production art/culture approval, full platform coverage, or manual human acceptance.

Before this QA handoff, git status showed allowed uncommitted route/state files produced by routing. They are not forbidden-path changes, but lead integration should reconcile and commit them with the QA handoff.

blockers.summary: Explicit product-owner decision is required before the task can be closed as content locked or any content lock state can be accepted.

requested_action: Route the QA-accepted package to product_owner for the formal content lock Human Gate decision. Do not close M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 as content locked, do not mark content_lock_accepted=true, and do not continue autonomous M7/M8 work until the owner decision is recorded.
